chunk_summary: Guidelines for vegetarian sick-day menus emphasize maintaining energy-dense, soft cereal-pulse meals, hydration with ORS, and recovery feeding with extra protein and energy for 2 weeks post-illness to support catch-up growth.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of continuing energy-dense feeding during illness in vegetarian diets.
Learn practical sick-day meal options for children with reduced appetite or vomiting.
Recognize the role of ORS and zinc in managing diarrhea while continuing feeding.
Implement recovery feeding strategies to promote catch-up growth post-illness.
key_facts: Continue breastfeeding and offer small, frequent, energy-dense feeds during illness.
Practical vegetarian sick-day meals include moong dal khichdi, soft daliya with dal, and curd rice with legumes.
ORS and zinc are essential for managing diarrhea; avoid prolonged 'rice water only' feeding.
Recovery feeding requires one extra energy- and protein-dense meal daily for 2 weeks post-illness.
Escalate care if the child shows lethargy, dehydration, or blood in stools.
reference_quote: Prolonged 'rice water only' worsens nutritional recovery; continue feeding during diarrhea and prioritize ORS and zinc per standard protocols.
chunk_heading: VEGETARIAN & REGIONAL FEEDING PATTERNS;SICK-DAY MENUS AND RECOVERY FEEDING;
heading_path: VEGETARIAN & REGIONAL FEEDING PATTERNS;SICK-DAY MENUS AND RECOVERY FEEDING;
